paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Jehovah spake unto Moses and to Aaron, saying,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, When any man hath an issue out of his flesh, because of his issue he is unclean.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And this shall be his uncleanness in his issue: whether his flesh run with his issue, or his flesh be stopped from his issue, it is his uncleanness.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Every bed whereon he that hath the issue lieth shall be unclean; and everything whereon he sitteth shall be unclean.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And whosoever toucheth his bed shall wash his clothes, and bathe himself in water, and be unclean until the even.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And he that sitteth on anything whereon he that hath the issue sat shall wash his clothes, and bathe himself in water, and be unclean until the even.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And he that toucheth the flesh of him that hath the issue shall wash his clothes, and bathe himself in water, and be unclean until the even.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And if he that hath the issue spit upon him that is clean, then he shall wash his clothes, and bathe himself in water, and be unclean until the even.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And what saddle soever he that hath the issue rideth upon shall be unclean.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And whosoever toucheth anything that was under him shall be unclean until the even: and he that beareth those things shall wash his clothes, and bathe himself in water, and be unclean until the even.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And whomsoever he that hath the issue toucheth, without having rinsed his hands in water, he shall wash his clothes, and bathe himself in water, and be unclean until the even.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And the earthen vessel, which he that hath the issue toucheth, shall be broken; and every vessel of wood shall be rinsed in water.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And when he that hath an issue is cleansed of his issue, then he shall number to himself seven days for his cleansing, and wash his clothes; and he shall bathe his flesh in running water, and shall be clean.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And on the eighth day he shall take to him two turtle-doves, or two young pigeons, and come before Jehovah unto the door of the tent of meeting, and give them unto the priest:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and the priest shall offer them, the one for a sin-offering, and the other for a burnt-offering; and the priest shall make atonement for him before Jehovah for his issue.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And if any man`s seed of copulation go out from him, then he shall bathe all his flesh in water, and be unclean until the even.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And every garment, and every skin, whereon is the seed of copulation, shall be washed with water, and be unclean until the even.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: The woman also with whom a man shall lie with seed of copulation, they shall both bathe themselves in water, and be unclean until the even.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And if a woman have an issue, [and] her issue in her flesh be blood, she shall be in her impurity seven days: and whosoever toucheth her shall be unclean until the even.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And everything that she lieth upon in her impurity shall be unclean: everything also that she sitteth upon shall be unclean.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And whosoever toucheth her bed shall wash his clothes, and bathe himself in water, and be unclean until the even.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And whosoever toucheth anything that she sitteth upon shall wash his clothes, and bathe himself in water, and be unclean until the even.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And if it be on the bed, or on anything whereon she sitteth, when he toucheth it, he shall be unclean until the even.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And if any man lie with her, and her impurity be upon him, he shall be unclean seven days; and every bed whereon he lieth shall be unclean.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And if a woman have an issue of her blood many days not in the time of her impurity, or if she have an issue beyond the time of her impurity; all the days of the issue of her uncleanness she shall be as in the days of her impurity: she is unclean.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Every bed whereon she lieth all the days of her issue shall be unto her as the bed of her impurity: and everything whereon she sitteth shall be unclean, as the uncleanness of her impurity.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And whosoever toucheth those things shall be unclean, and shall wash his clothes, and bathe himself in water, and be unclean until the even.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: But if she be cleansed of her issue, then she shall number to herself seven days, and after that she shall be clean.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And on the eighth day she shall take unto her two turtle-doves, or two young pigeons, and bring them unto the priest, to the door of the tent of meeting.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And the priest shall offer the one for a sin-offering, and the other for a burnt-offering; and the priest shall make atonement for her before Jehovah for the issue of her uncleanness.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Thus shall ye separate the children of Israel from their uncleanness, that they die not in their uncleanness, when they defile my tabernacle that is in the midst of them.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: This is the law of him that hath an issue, and of him whose seed of copulation goeth from him, so that he is unclean thereby;

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and of her that is sick with her impurity, and of him that hath an issue, of the man, and of the woman, and of him that lieth with her that is unclean.
